author: runner-1
rationale: Input pin only (P2 P-9: a PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder completed by ops amendment after the judge-r1p run and before the final-phase record; no design field is touched). The judge-r1p STAND-IN run (GPT-5.6-Sol via npx-pinned @openai/codex 0.144.1, reasoning effort low, isolated CODEX_HOME, subscription auth) completed all 50 real blinded review sheets in seed-pinned real_order, then 50 deranged-explication probes in probe_order, then 10 retest duplicates (preflight PASS on both calibration sheets first-attempt; 0 no-label; 0 transport retries; every per-call blinding grep clean; final blinding sweep CLEAN). data/g9-review-llmproxy/ was assembled: judge-r1p-responses.jsonl (50), judge-r1p-probe-responses.jsonl (50), judge-r1p-retest-responses.jsonl (10), labels-proxy.jsonl (50; composite = MEASURED mechanical legality AND substitutable=yes AND cross_translatable=yes), and summary.json (the exact analysis-input integers per analysis/g9_llmproxy.py). This ops amendment resolves the placeholder in pins.corpus_hashes to the kot-corpus-hash/1 digest of data/g9-review-llmproxy/ (tools/registry/corpus-pin.py g9-review-llmproxy). One-shot pin discipline: any change to those bytes before the final-phase run invalidates this value and requires a pre-run correction record, not a new ops amendment.
